home.headline: Fernando
home.tagline: Staff Software Developer · Professor · Content Creator
home.shortBio1: I have 20+ years of experience as a software developer specialized in web development. I care about community, doing things well, and pushing others to be a better version of themselves.
home.shortBio2: I am based in the San Francisco Bay Area. I was born and raised in Mexicali, México.
home.shortBio3: It is my privilege to currently teach an UX-centered course at Northeastern University, Oakland Campus.
home.profileImageAlt: A photo of Fernando López smiling and looking at the camera.

projects.title: Projects
projects.slug: A collection of experiments and explorations in engineering and design.

countries.title: The World's Countries
countries.slug: A quick reference and an experiment on static deployment.
countries.intro1: Before I loved web development, I loved geography. As a child, I used to buy  these yearly World Almanacs. This intends to be pretty much like that: a reference for the world's countries, with basic data to jumpstart a research session.
countries.intro2: This was created as an experiment to test the power of static websites. Data was consumed from the REST Countries API at compilation time. Due to API limitations, data is only available in English.

0.slug: How should the software developer think about their career development in an AI-driven job market? Explore the concept of the T-shaped developer and how it can help you drive your career effectively.
